items.q: Is a spinal reflex action controlled by the brain?
items.correct: Most spinal reflexes are processed by relay neurones in the spinal cord, so they occur without the brain making a decision.
items.misconception: Reflexes are quick decisions made consciously by the brain.
items.random_wrong: Reflexes are controlled by hormones carried in the blood.
items.why: Students assume every response involves conscious thought, missing that a reflex arc bypasses the brain for speed.

items.q: What is the difference between taxis and kinesis?
items.correct: Taxis is directional movement toward or away from a stimulus; kinesis is a non-directional change in speed or turning rate.
items.misconception: Taxis and kinesis both simply mean moving away from danger.
items.random_wrong: Taxis occurs in plants while kinesis occurs in animals.
items.why: The similar contexts lead students to treat both responses as identical directional movements.

items.q: Why does a shoot grow towards light?
items.correct: Auxin (IAA) accumulates on the shaded side and promotes cell elongation there, bending the shoot toward the light.
items.misconception: The shoot grows toward light because the light physically pulls the cells over.
items.random_wrong: The shoot grows toward light because chlorophyll is attracted to light.
items.why: Students imagine light pulling the plant rather than a hormone causing uneven growth.

items.q: The correct order of components in a simple reflex arc is:
items.correct: receptor, sensory neurone, relay neurone, motor neurone, effector
items.misconception: receptor, motor neurone, relay neurone, sensory neurone, effector
items.random_wrong: effector, motor neurone, relay neurone, sensory neurone, receptor
items.why: Correct answer: receptor, sensory neurone, relay neurone, motor neurone, effector. “receptor, motor neurone, relay neurone, sensory neurone, effector” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: A woodlouse moves faster in dry air and slows in damp air, with no set direction. This response is:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: kinesis. “positive taxis” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A plant shoot growing towards a light source is showing:
items.why: Correct answer: positive phototropism. “negative geotropism” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Receptors are described as transducers because they:
items.correct: convert the energy of a stimulus into a nerve impulse
items.misconception: amplify the size of the action potential
items.random_wrong: store information as a chemical memory
items.why: The answer examiners want is: convert the energy of a stimulus into a nerve impulse. “amplify the size of the action potential” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
